options.name: -b
options.description: Specify the logical page body lines to be numbered

options.name: -d
options.description:
Specify the delimiter characters used to indicate the
start of a logical page section in the input file. At most two
characters may be specified; if only one character is specified,
the first character is replaced and the second character remains unchanged

options.name: -f
options.description: Specify the same as -b type except for logical page footer lines

options.name: -h
options.description: Specify the same as -b type except for logical page header lines

options.name: -i
options.description: Specify the increment value used to number logical page lines

options.name: -l
options.description:
If numbering of all lines is specified for the current
logical section using the corresponding -b a, -f a or -h a option, specify
the number of adjacent blank lines to be considered as one. For example,
-l 2 results in only the second adjacent blank line being numbered

options.name: -n
options.description: Specify the line numbering output format

options.name: -p
options.description: Specify that line numbering should not be restarted at logical page delimiters

options.name: -s
options.description:
Specify the characters used in separating the line
number and the corresponding text line.  The default
sep setting is a single tab character

options.name: -v
options.description: Specify the initial value used to number logical page lines; see also the description of the -p option

options.name: -w
options.description:
Specify the number of characters to be occupied by the
line number; in case the width is insufficient to hold the line number,
it will be truncated to its width least significant digits
